rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FAY JEAN CHIN WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF STATUS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/26. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The petition does not disclose or request a specific amount of time the executor needs to file her petition for final distribution, which, it appears, should have been done in 1987 or so, had she filed the I&A timely. There is no need to appoint yet another probate referee as the executor apparently has had the Final I&A since late 1986. She should just file it and withdraw the later, reportedly incorrect I&A. (3) If Helen Fong's husband and administrator of her estate is now deceased, who is administering her estate? Has her estate been closed? How are her children her successors-in-interest? (4) It does not appear that notice of this estate administration was ever given to the Canadian consulate. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/2/26. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

(1) The proof of service filed on 4/14/26 still shows the incorrect (incomplete) title of the petition in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing. (2) Other issues with the accounting, posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 3/10/26, have not been addressed in an amendment to the accounting, signed and verified by the conservator. At the very least, a complete Schedule E for the accounting ending on 7/31/23 should be filed now that includes the number of shares of the securities on hand then. (3) A Court-appointed fiduciary must supplement or amend his own pleading(s), not the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] Deadline to provide the assigned examiner courtesy copies of any new filed papers is 6/2/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The conservator's deferred fees will likely not be approved later. This Court does not favor fees being deferred until long after the accounting period has passed during which services were provided.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Respondent, Martha Wash?S Notice Of Demurrer And Demurrer To Petitioner, Tanya Rockett?S Second Amended Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To Estate
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Michael B. McNaughton. Before the court is Martha Wash's Demurrer to Second Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate ("Demurrer"). Movant's request for judicial notice of various pleadings and documents filed in this matter is GRANTED, pursuant to Evid. Code secs. 452 and 453. The Demurrer is IN PART OVERRULED AND IN PART SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Movant previously demurred to Petitioner's Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate. On November 25, 2025, this court entered Order Sustaining In Part And Overruling In Part Demurrer To Amended Petition For Order Directing Transfer Of Possession Of Personal Property Belonging To The Estate. Petitioner timely filed Second Amended Petition for Order Directing Transfer of Possession of Personal Property Belonging to the Estate. Per the Notice of Demurrer, Movant now demurs to the Second Amended Petition ("SAP") under "California Code of Civil Procedure [secs.] 430.10(d), 430.10(e), and 430.10(f) as the Amended Petition contains a defect of parties, does not state facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action, and it is uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible. Petitioner's verified admissions bar recovery under the causes of action pled." The Notice of Demurrer now before the court is brought, verbatim, on the same grounds as the previous demurrer, which was sustained in part with leave to amend and overruled in part. Per the current Memorandum of Points and Authorities, Movant argues, The Court must sustain Respondent's demurrers because Petitioner has: (a) failed to set forth any new facts not alleged in her [First Amended Petition], and, aside from one inapplicable code section, has failed to set forth any new legal argument not previously stated in her FAP or her opposition to Respondent's previous Demurrer to the FAP; (b) failed to state facts sufficient to constitute any cause of action ("COA") and failed to plead all the necessary elements of each of her COAs (Code Civ. Proc., [sec.] 430.10(e)); and (c) each of the "COAs" are so unclear, uncertain, ambiguous, and unintelligible that Respondent cannot reasonably respond (Id., [sec.] 430.10(f)). Broadly speaking, Petitioner's arguments fail because: (1) they are contradicted by her judicial admissions and prior stipulations; (2) the law she cites is inapplicable to the claims she is trying to make; and/or (3) she cannot allege the facts necessary to establish the elements of her claims. MPA at 2:2-11. Movant also makes several assertions in footnotes as to why the prior demurrer was sustained, which are not reflected in the November 2025 Order or Minutes of the hearing.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of3] Demur to Fourth Cause of Action for damages and award of attorney's fees and costs pursuant to Probate Code section 859, on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is OVERRULED. The prior demurrer to this cause of action was brought on the same grounds, and was overruled. Although the pleading contains legal argument and conclusions, the pleading also states material facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. A further amended petition shall be filed within ten days of entry of this order. Movant shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GLORIA JEAN WASH RILEY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purposes Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] When considering a demurrer, the trial court must accept as true all material facts pleaded in the complaint and those arising by reasonable implication, cannot consider the party's ability to prove their factual allegations (Moore v. Conliffe (1994) 7 Cal. 4th 634, 638; Montclair Parkowners Ass'n v. City of Montclair (1999) 76 Cal. App. 4th 784.; Sheehan v. San Francisco 49ers, Ltd. (2009) Cal.4th 992, 998, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) Demurrers test legal sufficiency based on the allegations as pleaded, without regard to the plaintiff's ability to prove the alleged facts. (see, Gray v. Dignity Health (2021) 70 Cal.App.5th 225.) Demurrers challenge only the legal sufficiency of pleadings on their face, not the truth or adequacy of factual allegations or evidence (Code of Civil Proc. sec. 430.30(a)). The sole question raised by demurrer is whether the pleading is facially-not factually-deficient. Movant's general demurrer on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure sec. 430.10(d), a defect or misjoinder of parties, is OVERRULED. The prior demurrer was sustained on this basis with leave to amend and the SAP now alleges the capacities in which Movant is named as a respondent. Movant's general demurrer under Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(f) for uncertainty is OVERRULED. As the court ruled on the previous demurrer to the pleadings on these grounds, the Petition is sufficiently pleaded to apprise Movant of the allegations being made against her, in her capacities as pleaded, and the relief sought. (See, Butler v. Sequeira (1950) 100 C.A.2d 143, 145-146.) Demur to First Cause of Action brought under Probate Code section 850(a)(2)) and seeking return of funds to the Estate, on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is OVERRULED. Although the pleading contains legal argument and conclusions, the pleading also states material facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. Demur to Second Cause of Action brought under Probate Code section 13111(a) and seeking return of funds to the Estate, on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is OVERRULED. Although the pleading contains legal argument and conclusions, the pleading also states material facts sufficient to constitute a cause of action. Demur to Third Cause of Action seeking treble damages under Probate Code section 13111(b), on the basis of Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e) is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. Under the statute, "[i]f the transferee fraudulently secured the payment, delivery, or transfer of the decedent's property under this chapter, the transferee is liable under this section for restitution of three times the fair market value of the transferred property." (Probate Code sec. 13111(b). Facially, the pleading states, "Absent more information, Petitioner does not contend that Martha secured the AIG Funds belonging to the Estate by fraud." (SAP at par. 53.) Instead, the pleading alleges Respondent fraudulently retained the estate assets after the personal administrator requested transfer of those estate assets. Facially, this is not an adequate pleading of a claim pursuant to Probate Code section 13111(b). If Petitioner has since obtained information sufficient to allege Respondent secured the payment, delivery, or transfer of estate assets by fraud, Petitioner may, with specificity, do so in a further amended pleading.

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE AMENDED AND RESTATED MALEE TANG TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/26. (No earlier availability.) (1) Proof of the additional bond ordered on 11/27/24 still has not been filed. (2) Based on the FMV of assets on hand at the end of the accounting period (7/31/24) even the increased bond ordered on 11/27/24 appears to be insufficient. Neither Trustee nor his attorney has filed an ex parte petition to increase bond. [CRC 7.204.] (3) A Court-appointed fiduciary must supplement or amend his own pleading(s), not the attorney. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (4) If the values of the assets received from the conservatorship are not the carry values on hand in the conservatorship estate at the time of transfer, assets must be appraised by a probate referee. (5) Why did Gordon Tang provide personal funds to the trust? Is he expecting to be reimbursed? There is no liabilities schedule. (6) Transfers of cash between accounts are not changes in the form of assets that need to be shown on an informational schedule. Deadline to provide the assigned examiner and courtesy copies of any new filed papers is 6/2/26. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN DELWIN DUSENBURY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. NB: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed. And there was no order dated 2/8/25 that dispensed with the requirement of a probate referee. Notice to the DHCS and FTB should be given by the general personal representative (not before one has been appointed). The issue of a surviving child are not heirs entitled to notice. A schedule showing the change in the form of assets is not required when accounting has been waived; the reported gains and losses on sales is sufficient.
